Package i5.cb.api

Class CBterm

  • java.lang.Object
    • i5.cb.api.CBterm

  • public class CBterm
extends java.lang.Object

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      CBterm​(java.lang.String term)
      Construct a CBterm out of the given string.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      CBterm getArg​(int i)
      Gets one argument of the term.
      CBterm[] getArgs​()
      Gets the arguments of the term.
      java.lang.String getFunctor​()
      Gets the functor of the term.
      int getListLength​()
      Get the length of the list, if the term is a list.
      boolean isConstant​()
      Test if the term is a constant (a functor with no arguments).
      boolean isList​()
      Test if the term is a list.
      static void main​(java.lang.String[] argv)

      Get the string representation of the term.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • CBterm

        public CBterm​(java.lang.String term)
       throws CBtermParserException
        Construct a CBterm out of the given string. The String is parsed and the members of the resulting CBterm are initialized according to the contents of the string.
        Parameters:
        term - the string to parse
        Throws:
        CBtermParserException - is thrown if an error occurs during parsing

    • Method Detail

      • getFunctor

        public java.lang.String getFunctor​()
        Gets the functor of the term.
        Returns:
        the functor as string or null if the term has no functor

      • getArgs

        public CBterm[] getArgs​()
        Gets the arguments of the term.
        Returns:
        the arguments stored in an array of CBterm (may have length=0 if there are no arguments)

      • getArg

        public CBterm getArg​(int i)
        Gets one argument of the term.
        Parameters:
        i - the argument position.
        Returns:
        the argument
        Throws:
        java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if i is greater than the number of arguments

      • isList

        public boolean isList​()
        Test if the term is a list.
        Returns:
        true if the term is a list.

      • isConstant

        public boolean isConstant​()
        Test if the term is a constant (a functor with no arguments).
        Returns:
        true if the term is a functor.

      • getListLength

        public int getListLength​()
        Get the length of the list, if the term is a list.
        Returns:
        the length of the list, or -1 if the term is not a list.

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString​()
        Get the string representation of the term.
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        the string

      • main

        public static void main​(java.lang.String[] argv)
                 throws java.lang.Exception
        Main Method for testing only
        Parameters:
        argv - a term as string
        Throws:
        java.lang.Exception
